texto: I just finished running, and I look and smell like nothing very pleasant. Why, oh, why did he have to bump into me now?
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Abigail Owen: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a humor. O trecho “I just finished running, and I look and smell like nothing very pleasant. Why, oh, why did he hav…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que humor, cute, funny pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de funny e random.

frase_original: I just finished running, and I look and smell like nothing very pleasant. Why, oh, why did he have to bump into me now?

texto: She'd read ton of books with female heroines who swooned at the sight of their true love and had thought them to be incredibly wimpy. Now here she stood, barely able to keep herself upright. Not that she was in love...far from it. But a girl could appreciate a bona fide hottie when she saw one, right?
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Abigail Owen que provoca vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. Ao questionar “She'd read ton of books with female heroines who swooned at the sight of their true love and had …”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre humor, cute, funny, com foco em funny e love.
